title: About Me
html:
My name is Marinda Engelbrecht and I grew up on a farm just outside Durbanville. I always tell people that I could churn butter before I could read.  My growing-up years were carefree. Those were years of playing outside until you collapsed from tiredness at night.

The coal stove in our house was a central point.  In front of it we bathed in winter evenings in the zinc bath.  My mother hung our vests and pyjamas over her little doors to warm up.  In front of the coal stove we churned butter with the red-topped churn.  Our little children's eyes would light up as the golden yellow rose to the top of the milk. That was reward enough.

I come from a family where food was more than just something you ate.  Food was for comfort, for talking, for debating, for celebrating and just simply for enjoyment too.  My mother cooked with what was available.  Jam was made according to the season.  I remember the big, shiny pots with jam bubbling away.  How my mother skimmed off the foam and then finally bottled it in the canning jars. That was our wealth. Shiny bottles on the kitchen cupboard.

I would love to share my love of cooking with you.  This is no fancy cooking blog.  It's simply a straightforward cooking blog so that my children and their friends and their friends' friends can learn to cook. So that they don't have to call me so often to ask: how do you make…

I hope my love of soul food will help you too to simply enjoy cooking.

Regards,
Marinda

If you have any requests for recipes or dishes, please let me know. I'm happy to help.

You can do this in one of three ways:

  1. In a comment on the website.
  2. By sending an email to marindakook@gmail.com.
  3. By writing on the Facebook page.
